title: Two officers rescue woman found asleep amid house fire in Merrimac
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Two Merrimac officers rescued a sleeping woman from a house fire early Wednesday morning.
Officers DeCoste and White entered through a second entrance after smoke blocked their initial entry.
The fire was reported by Jeff Strogney, who saw flames up to 15 feet tall and dialed 911.

title: A 22-year-old man died in a three-car crash in Canton
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A 22-year-old Marshfield man, Mathew McTiernan, died in a three-car crash on I-95 in Canton.
The crash, involving a Toyota sedan, occurred at 6:20 a.m. and affected two of three traffic lanes.
Massachusetts State Police from Milton Barracks are investigating the cause of the crash in Canton.

title: Baby boomers feeling crushed by housing crunch
author: Lindsay Crudele
bullets: Baby boomers represented 53% of home sellers and 42% of buyers in 2025, according to National Association of Realtors data.
Rising housing costs in Massachusetts affect aging residents, with baby boomers now comprising 23% of the state's population.
Southbridge's Lebanon Hill Housing complex residents face rent hikes up to 70%, following Shorelight Real Estate's acquisition.

title: Natick mom to run the 2026 Boston Marathon for kinder communities
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Danica Champion, a Natick ESL teacher, will run her first marathon at the 2026 Boston Marathon.
Champion is raising funds for Spark Kindness, supporting positive youth development in Natick communities.
She advocates for addressing bullying and discrimination in schools, emphasizing the importance of kindness and resilience.

title: Developer vows to present more ‘options’ for Davis Square tower project amid neighborhood opposition
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Copper Mill plans to present new options for a 26-story tower in Davis Square after neighborhood opposition.
The development includes 502 apartments, with 126 affordable units, and no planned parking, sparking criticism.
Residents oppose the project's impact on Davis Square businesses, while Copper Mill plans a project office at 235 Elm St.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for his 2 nephews with down syndrome
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Steven Ponte from Somerville is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for his nephews with Down syndrome.
Ponte's twin nephews, Sebastian and Zachary, inspired his participation in honor of Sebastian's memory and Zachary's future.
Ponte is running to support the Massachusetts Down Syndrome Congress, a crucial support for families with Down syndrome.

title: Boston firefighter, National Guard member reportedly injured in strike that killed 6 U.S. soldiers
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston firefighter and National Guard Major Mark Garcia was injured in an Iranian drone strike in Kuwait.
The drone strike killed six U.S. soldiers stationed in Kuwait; Garcia is recovering in a German hospital.
Gov. Maura Healey discussed Garcia's injuries and the Boston Firefighters union expressed pride in his service.

title: Lowell murder trial delayed after lead State Police investigator allegedly causes fatal crash while driving drunk
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Lowell murder retrial delayed due to lead investigator's involvement in fatal 2023 crash while allegedly drunk.
Sgt. Scott Quigley, involved in the crash, investigated the 2020 murder of Tyrone Phet allegedly by the Phan brothers.
Justice Christopher Barry-Smith postponed the April 27 trial citing unresolved investigations into Quigley's actions.

title: Watch: Body cam shows Patriots party at downtown Boston restaurant
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Body camera footage shows Patriots partying at Estella restaurant with hookahs and strippers after AFC championship.
Police found multiple liquor bottles, hookahs, and partially nude women as they responded to after-hours party complaints.
Restaurant owner Helder George Brandao faces a three-day license suspension for violating smoking and entertainment regulations.

title: 4 things to know about new Patriots safety Kevin Byard
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots sign safety Kevin Byard to a one-year, $9 million deal, adding a veteran leader to their secondary.
Byard has 36 career interceptions in the NFL and led the league with seven in 2025 while playing for the Bears.
Byard, 32, has never missed an NFL game due to injury, demonstrating remarkable durability over his ten-year career.
